title: Architecture overview
description: System boundaries, service roles, and how the selected repos fit together.
prompt:
Provide a PE technical diligence architecture overview of the selected repositories.

Cover:
1. Purpose of each repo and its role in the product
2. Major runtime components (APIs, workers, UIs, shared libs)
3. How services communicate (HTTP, queues, sockets, shared DB)
4. External dependencies and third-party platforms
5. Deployment / environment assumptions visible in the code

Cite concrete file paths. Flag uncertainties explicitly. Do not propose code changes.

title: Security review
description: AuthN/Z, secrets handling, injection surfaces, and data exposure risks.
prompt:
Perform a read-only security review of the selected repositories for PE diligence.

Assess:
1. Authentication and authorization patterns
2. Secret / credential handling
3. Input validation and injection risks
4. Sensitive data flows (PII, payments, tokens)
5. Notable insecure defaults or missing controls

For each finding: severity (High/Med/Low), evidence (file path), and business impact. Cite sources. No code edits.

title: Data flow & persistence
description: Where data lives, how it moves, and retention/ownership signals.
prompt:
Map data flows across the selected repositories.

Include:
1. Primary data stores (Mongo, SQL, S3, caches, etc.)
2. Key entities / collections and owning services
3. Inbound/outbound integrations that move customer or payment data
4. Logging / analytics destinations if visible
5. Data residency or multi-tenant isolation clues

Cite files. Call out unknowns. Read-only analysis only.

title: Ops & reliability
description: Deployability, observability, failure modes, and operational maturity.
prompt:
Assess operational maturity of the selected repositories for PE diligence.

Cover:
1. Build / deploy / CI signals in-repo
2. Health checks, metrics, logging, tracing
3. Retry, timeout, and failure-handling patterns
4. Background jobs / cron / queues
5. Obvious single points of failure

Cite evidence. Distinguish confirmed vs inferred. No changes.

title: Vendor lock-in
description: Third-party platforms that would be costly to replace.
prompt:
Identify vendor lock-in and concentration risk in the selected repositories.

List major third-party dependencies (cloud, auth, payments, messaging, AI, media, etc.). For each: where used, how deeply embedded, and switching difficulty (High/Med/Low) with rationale. Cite files. Read-only.

title: Key-person & maintainability
description: Bus factor, undocumented complexity, and maintainability risks.
prompt:
Assess key-person and maintainability risk from the codebase alone.

Look for:
1. Highly concentrated complexity (god modules, sparse docs)
2. Custom frameworks / one-off patterns
3. Missing tests around critical paths
4. Undocumented operational runbooks implied by code
5. Areas that would be hard for a new team to own quickly

Cite files. Be explicit about confidence. Ask/read-only only.
